steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which quotation best supports the claim that the storyteller's stories are based on personal experiences. The correct quotation must explicitly show that the stories come from the speaker's own life rather than from outside sources.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Never got her stories out of any book at all, / But that they came / Right out of her own life.': directly contrasts books (external) with her own life (personal) — the clearest evidence the stories are based on lived experience. ✓
  • Choice B: focuses on the atmosphere or setting of storytelling, not the origin of the stories. ✗
  • Choice C: describes the storyteller's voice or manner, not where the stories come from. ✗
  • Choice D: describes a physical or narrative setting rather than the source of the stories. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
